chapter_title.en: Chapter on Whose Testimony Must Be Rejected and Whose Testimony Must Be Accepted

text: 3281 - رُوِيَ عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ قَالَ : سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَمَّا يُرَدُّ مِنَ اَلشُّهُودِ فَقَالَ "اَلظَّنِينُ وَ اَلْمُتَّهَمُ وَ اَلْخَصْمُ" قَالَ قُلْتُ فَالْفَاسِقُ وَ اَلْخَائِنُ قَالَ "هَذَا يَدْخُلُ فِي اَلظَّنِينِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3281 - It is narrated from Ubaydullah ibn Ali Al-Halabi, who said:
Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about the categories of witnesses whose testimony is to be rejected.
Imam (as) replied: "The one suspected (Al-Zaneen), the accused (Al-Muttaham), and the adversary (Al-Khasm)."
I said: "What about the immoral (Al-Fasiq) and the dishonest (Al-Kha'in)?"
Imam (as) said: "These fall under the category of the suspected (Al-Zaneen)."
